Label: X36623929
Authors: Chu C; Liu S; He N; Zeng Z; Wang J; Zhang Z; Zeljic K; van der Stelt O; Sun B; Yan F; Liu C; Li D; Zhang C
Network_Areas: Caudate; Cerebellar hemisphere; Cerebellar vermis; Globus Pallidus externa (GPe); Globus Pallidus interna (GPi); Key motor areas; Motor thalamus; Paracentral lobule; Postcentral gyrus; Precentral gyrus; Putamen; Subthalamic Nucleus (STN); Supplementary Motor Area (SMA)
State_Features: Dynamic FC variance; Dwell time; Fraction time; Frequency; State-transition probability
Identified_States_Description: Activation-based states: States 1-3 (Positive activation); State 4 (Mean activation); States 5-8 (Negative activation)
Integration_State_Findings: Increased coverage in states with positive motor network activation is related to improved motor performance.
Segregation_State_Findings: STN-DBS reduced abnormal segregation and low connectivity in motor network
State_Pattern_Conclusion: Integration associated with better outcomes / Segregation with worse
Transition_Findings: Increased transition probabilities are related to improved motor performance scores.
Transition_Pattern_Conclusion: More transitions associated with better outcomes
Proposed_Biomarker_Summary_Cleaned: STN-DBS modulates whole-brain motor network states; providing a mechanistic explanation for its therapeutic effects on motor symptoms.
Limitations: Motor-region focus; DBS-induced transitions hard to interpret; Limited temporal resolution

State_Features: Not applicable

Integration_State_Findings: Higher integration is related to higher tremor amplitude.
Segregation_State_Findings: Not Applicable
State_Pattern_Conclusion: Integration associated with worse outcomes / Segregation with better

Label: X39243826
Authors: Shen B; Yao Q; Li W; Dong S; Zhang H; Zhao Y; Pan Y; Jiang X; Li D; Chen Y; Yan J; Zhang W; Zhu Q; Zhang D; Zhang L; Wu Y
Network_Areas: Basal Ganglia (BG); Cerebellum (CB); Sensorimotor Network (SMN)
State_Features: Dwell time; Fraction time; Number of transitions
Identified_States_Description: State 4 (Integrated; least frequent); State 2 (Segregated; most frequent); States 1 and 3 (Intermediate)
Integration_State_Findings: TDPD patients spent less mean dwell time in highly interconnected state 4.
Segregation_State_Findings: PD is associated with spending more time in a segregated state.
State_Pattern_Conclusion: Integration associated with better outcomes / Segregation with worse
Transition_Findings: A higher number of transitions is related to better motor performance.
Transition_Pattern_Conclusion: More transitions associated with better outcomes
Proposed_Biomarker_Summary_Cleaned: Persisting in sparse-connectivity (segregated) states is related to greater tremor severity. Hyperconnectivity between the Cerebellar (CB) and Sensorimotor (SMN) networks may be compensatory.
Limitations: ON state only; Small sample

Label: X36229900
Authors: Si Q; Gan C; Zhang H; Cao X; Sun H; Wang M; Wang L; Yuan Y; Zhang K
Network_Areas: Auditory Network (AUD); Basal Ganglia (BG); Central Executive Network (CEN); Cerebellum (CB); Default Mode Network (DMN); Sensorimotor Network (SMN); Visual Network (VIS)
State_Features: Dwell time; Fraction time; Number of transitions
Identified_States_Description: State 1 (Integrated; least frequent); State 5 (Segregated; most frequent); States 2-4 (Intermediate with specific network features)
Integration_State_Findings: More time spent in an integrated state is related to lower motor performance (appearance of LID) and severer dyskinesia.
Segregation_State_Findings: Not Applicable
State_Pattern_Conclusion: Integration associated with worse outcomes / Segregation with better
Proposed_Biomarker_Summary_Cleaned: Dyskinesia in PD may be explained by weak inhibition of motor loops by the Cognitive Control Network (CEN) combined with over-excitation in VIS and SMN.
Limitations: ON state only

Label: X35237143
Authors: Xu J; Yu M; Wang H; Li Y; Li L; Ren J; Pan C; Liu W
Network_Areas: Auditory Network (AUD); Basal Ganglia (BG); Central Executive Network (CEN); Default Mode Network (DMN); Sensorimotor Network (SMN); Visual Network (VIS)
State_Features: Dwell time; Fraction time; Number of transitions
Graph_Measures: Betweenness centrality; Clustering coefficient; Global efficiency; Local efficiency; Small-worldness
Identified_States_Description: State 3 (Integrated; least frequent); State 1 (Segregated; most frequent); State 2 (DMN-dominated); State 4 (VIS-SMN connected)
Integration_State_Findings: Spending more time in a DMN-related state is associated with dementia.
Segregation_State_Findings: Not Applicable
State_Pattern_Conclusion: Neutral/Descriptive Finding
Proposed_Biomarker_Summary_Cleaned: Depression in PD may be linked to time-varying disruptions between the DMN and CEN; as well as abnormal dynamic graph metrics.
Limitations: Small sample; Short scan; Disease duration uncontrolled

Label: X.WOS.000451095400001
Authors: Engels; Gwenda; Vlaar; Annemarie; McCoy; Bronagh; Scherder; Erik; Douw; Linda
Data_Source_Institution: VU University Medical Center; OLVG Hospital; Zaans Medical Center
Network_Areas: Default Mode Network (DMN); Fronto-parietal Network (FPN)
State_Features: Dwell time; Fraction time; Number of transitions
Identified_States_Description: Not applicable (Method does not define states)
Integration_State_Findings: DMN dynamics are related to better memory; a finding also observed in PD patients without Mild Cognitive Impairment.
Segregation_State_Findings: Not Applicable
State_Pattern_Conclusion: Neutral/Descriptive Finding
Proposed_Biomarker_Summary_Cleaned: Dynamic connectivity of the Default Mode Network (DMN) is specifically linked to cognitive function in PD.
Limitations: Small sample; Disease duration uncontrolled; State mismatch (scan OFF vs test ON); Head-motion artifacts

Label: X33852889
Authors: Gan C; Ma K; Wang L; Si Q; Wang M; Yuan Y; Zhang K
Network_Areas: Auditory Network (AUD); Basal Ganglia (BG); Central Executive Network (CEN); Cerebellum (CB); Default Mode Network (DMN); Salience Network (SAL); Sensorimotor Network (SMN); Visual Network (VIS)
State_Features: Dwell time; Fraction time; Number of transitions
Identified_States_Description: State 3 (Integrated); State 4 (Segregated; most frequent); States 1 and 2 (Intermediate; network-specific)
Integration_State_Findings: PD patients spend less time in an integrated state compared to HCs.
Segregation_State_Findings: PD with REM sleep behavior disorder (RBD) spend more time in a segregated state than those without RBD.
State_Pattern_Conclusion: Integration associated with better outcomes / Segregation with worse
Proposed_Biomarker_Summary_Cleaned: Weaker connectivity between VIS-DMN; DMN-BG; and within the DMN in the most segregated state may be a neural substrate for REM Sleep Behavior Disorder (RBD) in PD.
Limitations: Head-motion artifacts; Many temporal derivatives applied

Authors: Cao Y; Si Q; Tong R; Zhang X; Li C; Mao S
Network_Areas: Attention Network (ATT); Auditory Network (AUD); Basal Ganglia (BG); Default Mode Network (DMN); Fronto-parietal Network (FPN); Sensorimotor Network (SMN); Visual Network (VIS)
State_Features: Dwell time; Fraction time; Number of transitions
Identified_States_Description: State 4 (Integrated; least frequent); State 3 (Segregated; most frequent); States 1 and 2 (Intermediate; network-specific)
Integration_State_Findings: Not Applicable
Segregation_State_Findings: Higher time spent in segregated states is related to worse motor and non-motor symptoms.
State_Pattern_Conclusion: Neutral/Descriptive Finding
Proposed_Biomarker_Summary_Cleaned: As PD progresses; patients spend more time in hypo-coupled (segregated) states; which parallels the worsening of non-motor symptoms.

Label: X39996352
Authors: Su D; Ji L; Cui Y; Gan L; Ma H; Liu Z; Duan Y; Stoessl AJ; Zhou J; Wu T; Liu Y; Feng T
Data_Source_Institution: Beijing Tiantan Hospital; Capital Medical University
Network_Areas: Auditory Network (AUD); Basal Ganglia (BG); Central Executive Network (CEN); Default Mode Network (DMN); Dorsal Attention Network (DAN); Fronto-parietal Network (FPN); Salience Network (SAL); Sensorimotor Network (SMN); Visual Network (VIS)
State_Features: Dwell time; Fraction time; Number of transitions
Identified_States_Description: State 4 (Integrated; least frequent); State 1 (Segregated; most frequent); States 2 and 3 (Intermediate with DMN/FPN vs VIS/DAN focus)
Integration_State_Findings: More severe FOG and worse cognitive performance are related to positive correlation between VIS and higher-order networks (DMN; FPN).
Segregation_State_Findings: State I described as “most frequent and relatively sparsely connected,” indicating a more weakly connected (sparser) configuration recurrent in the data.
State_Pattern_Conclusion: Neutral/Descriptive Finding
Transition_Findings: A higher number of transitions is related to an absence of FOG and better motor performance.
Transition_Pattern_Conclusion: More transitions associated with better outcomes
Proposed_Biomarker_Summary_Cleaned: The dynamics of visuomotor networks can track the presence and severity of Freezing of Gait (FOG) in PD.
Limitations: Short scan; PD–HC age mismatch

Label: X34557085
Authors: Zhu J; Zeng Q; Shi Q; Li J; Dong S; Lai C; Cheng G
Network_Areas: Basal Ganglia (BG); Default Mode Network (DMN); Fronto-parietal Network (FPN); Ventral Sensorimotor Network (SMN-v); Dorsal Sensorimotor Network (SMN-d)
State_Features: Dwell time; Fraction time; Number of transitions
Identified_States_Description: State 4 (Integrated; least frequent); State 1 (Segregated; most frequent); States 2 and 3 (Intermediate with specific network features)
Integration_State_Findings: Integrated states are associated with better motor performance.
Segregation_State_Findings: Tremor-dominant patients spend more time in a segregated state; which is related to poorer motor performance. The study also found higher global efficiency in PD.
State_Pattern_Conclusion: Mixed/Contradictory Findings
Proposed_Biomarker_Summary_Cleaned: Tremor severity is linked to longer dwell time in the segregated state and higher connectivity between the Basal Ganglia (BG) and ventral Sensorimotor (vSMN) networks.
Limitations: Small sample; OFF state only; Poor head-motion control

Label: X31280293
Authors: Fiorenzato E; Strafella AP; Kim J; Schifano R; Weis L; Antonini A; Biundo R
Data_Source_Institution: Neurology Clinic Padua; San Camillo Hospital Venice
Network_Areas: Auditory Network (AUD); Basal Ganglia (BG); Central Executive Network (CEN); Cerebellum (CB); Default Mode Network (DMN); Sensorimotor Network (SMN); Visual Network (VIS)
State_Features: Dwell time; Fraction time; Number of transitions
Identified_States_Description: State 2 (Integrated; less frequent); State 1 (Segregated; more frequent)
Integration_State_Findings: PD patients show lower time in integrated states compared to HCs.
Segregation_State_Findings: PD patients show higher time in segregated states. Worse cognitive performance is associated with long time spent in the segregated state.
State_Pattern_Conclusion: Integration associated with better outcomes / Segregation with worse
Transition_Findings: HCs have a higher number of transitions. Transitions are positively correlated with cognitive performance.
Transition_Pattern_Conclusion: More transitions associated with better outcomes
Proposed_Biomarker_Summary_Cleaned: Major cognitive alterations in PD are associated with spending longer in sparse (segregated) states; having fewer state transitions; and spending less time in integrated states.
Limitations: ON state only; Short scan; Low field strength

Label: X39523453
Authors: Shen B; Yao Q; Zhang Y; Jiang Y; Wang Y; Jiang X; Zhao Y; Zhang H; Dong S; Li D; Chen Y; Pan Y; Yan J; Han F; Li S; Zhu Q; Zhang D; Zhang L; Wu YC
Network_Areas: Basal Ganglia (BG); Central Executive Network (CEN); Cerebellum (CB); Default Mode Network (DMN); Sensorimotor Network (SMN)
State_Features: Dwell time; Fraction time; Number of transitions
Identified_States_Description: State 4 (Integrated; least frequent); State 1 (Segregated; most frequent); States 2 and 3 (Intermediate)
Integration_State_Findings: PIGD patients show reduced dwell time in the integrated state compared to non-PIGD. PD patients spend less time in integrated states than HCs.
Segregation_State_Findings: Higher time spent in the segregated state is related to higher PIGD scores (worse motor performance). PD patients spend more time in segregated states than HCs.
State_Pattern_Conclusion: Integration associated with better outcomes / Segregation with worse
Transition_Findings: The number of transitions is higher in non-PIGD than in PIGD patients. More transitions are related to better motor performance.
Transition_Pattern_Conclusion: More transitions associated with better outcomes
Proposed_Biomarker_Summary_Cleaned: Postural Instability and Gait Disorder (PIGD) is associated with reduced overall connectivity. Increased connectivity between the Cerebellum (CB) and DMN may be a dynamic compensatory mechanism.
Limitations: Not reported

Label: X33404161
Authors: Chen L; Bedard P; Hallett M; Horovitz SG
Exclusion_Other: 4 PD patients and 4 healthy volunteers excluded due to excess head movements during the scan
Network_Areas: Cerebellum (CB); Cingulo-opercular Network (CON); Fronto-parietal Network (FPN); Sensorimotor Network (SMN)
State_Features: Dwell time; Fraction time; Number of transitions
Identified_States_Description: Three balanced frequency states: State 1 (CB-SMN focused); State 2 (FPN-SMN focused); State 3 (CON-SMN focused)
Integration_State_Findings: HCs and PD-ON patients spend more time in a state with strong FPN-SMN connections compared to PD-OFF.
Segregation_State_Findings: The distinction between integrated/segregated states was not evident in the 3-state solution.
State_Pattern_Conclusion: Neutral/Descriptive Finding
Proposed_Biomarker_Summary_Cleaned: Dopamine depletion is linked to reduced dwell time and occurrence of an FPN-SMN connected state; Levodopa partially normalizes these dynamics.
Limitations: Small sample; Head-motion artifacts
